projects.name: BrgyLink
projects.shortDescription: A simple information and service portal designed for barangays, built using Bootstrap 5 and ASP.NET Core Razor Pages with C#. The project provides a user-friendly interface for barangay management and services.
projects.description:
The Barangay Information & Service Portal is a responsive web-based system designed to simplify barangay operations and improve access to public services. It is built with ASP.NET Core Razor Pages, C#, Bootstrap 5, and a secure role-based access structure.

Key Features:

• Resident Management — Maintain resident profiles, personal information, household details, and residency status.

• Document Services — Process applications for barangay clearances, certificates of residency, and other official documents.

• Blotter Management — Record, monitor, and manage reported incidents for improved public safety and case tracking.

• Payment System — Accept and monitor service payments through GCash and other supported payment methods.

• Reports — Generate reports for resident records, incidents, document requests, and financial transactions.

• AI FAQ Chatbot — Provide residents with automated answers to frequently asked questions at any time.

• User Access Control — Secure sensitive information using roles and permissions for administrators, barangay officials, staff, and residents.

• Announcements — Publish important news, schedules, emergency notices, and community updates.

• Searchable Records — Locate residents, documents, incidents, and transactions using multiple search criteria.

• Responsive Design — Provide a user-friendly experience across desktop computers, tablets, and mobile devices.
projects.preview: A simple information and service portal designed for barangays, built using Bootstrap 5 and ASP.NET Core Razor Pages with C#. The project provides a user-friendly interface for barangay management and services.
projects.challenge: One man developer, Lack of time due to OJT.
projects.solution: Managed my time and split tasks that is assigned day by day

blogs.title: A Day in the Life of a Software Developer
blogs.excerpt:
My day begins at 4:00 AM with a few hours of online gaming. At 8:00 AM, I take a shower and prepare for work before arriving at the office at around 9:00 AM. During my workday, I check my assigned tasks, develop features, investigate problems, fix bugs, test my solutions, and communicate with my teammates.

I leave the office at 6:00 PM and usually go jogging to stay active and reduce stress. After jogging, I return home, cook dinner, and sometimes watch a movie. Depending on how tired I feel, I go to sleep at either 9:00 PM or 11:00 PM.

My routine gives me a healthy balance between software development, entertainment, exercise, household responsibilities, relaxation, and rest.
paragraphs: A Day in the Life of a Software Developer

Being a software developer is not only about writing code. It also involves solving problems, learning new things, fixing unexpected issues, and finding a healthy balance between work and personal life. Every workday can bring a different challenge, but I usually follow a simple daily routine that helps me stay productive and relaxed.
Starting My Day Early
My day begins earlier than most people’s. I usually wake up at around 4:00 AM. The surroundings are still quiet at this time, which gives me a few peaceful hours before I need to prepare for work.

Instead of immediately checking work-related messages or opening my programming tools, I spend this time relaxing. I usually play online games with friends or other players. Gaming allows me to enjoy myself, communicate with different people, and temporarily forget about work responsibilities.

Some people may think waking up at 4:00 AM just to play games is unusual, but it is one of the activities that helps me start the day in a good mood. It gives me personal time before the busy part of my day begins.
Preparing for Work
At around 8:00 AM, I stop playing and start getting ready for the office. I take a shower, get dressed, prepare the things I need, and make sure that I am ready for the day ahead.
This part of my morning routine helps me shift from relaxation mode into work mode. After preparing everything, I travel to the office and usually arrive at around 9:00 AM.

When I reach the office, I settle into my workspace, turn on my computer, and check for tasks that need to be completed. I may review messages, bug reports, requests, or updates from other members of the team. This gives me an idea of what I need to prioritize for the day.
Beginning My Work as a Developer

Once I understand my assigned tasks, I begin working on them. My responsibilities can change depending on the needs of the project. On some days, I may develop a new feature for a system. On other days, I may need to investigate and fix an existing problem.

Fixing software issues is not always simple. Sometimes, the reported problem is easy to reproduce and solve. However, there are also situations where an error only happens under certain conditions. In these cases, I need to carefully examine the code, review logs, check the data, and test different possible solutions.

A large part of software development involves problem-solving. Writing the code is only one part of the process. I also need to understand what caused the issue, determine the best solution, and make sure that my changes do not negatively affect other parts of the system.
Testing and Improving My Work
After making changes to the code, I test the system to confirm that everything works correctly. Testing is important because a small change can sometimes create another problem in a different module.

If the issue is still present, I return to the code and continue investigating. This process can happen several times before I find the correct solution. It requires patience, attention to detail, and logical thinking.
When I successfully fix a difficult problem, I feel a strong sense of accomplishment. It is satisfying to see a feature work correctly after spending hours analyzing and improving it.

I may also communicate with my teammates throughout the day. We discuss requirements, report our progress, ask questions, and help one another when someone encounters a difficult issue. Software development is often considered an individual activity, but teamwork and clear communication are also important parts of the job.
Finishing the Workday

My workday usually ends at around 6:00 PM. Before leaving the office, I review what I have completed and make sure that my work is properly saved. If a task is unfinished, I take note of my progress so I can continue working on it the next day.

After spending several hours sitting and looking at a computer screen, I am usually ready to step away from technology for a while. Leaving the office gives me an opportunity to relax mentally and focus on something different.
Exercising After Work

After work, I usually go jogging. This is an important part of my routine because software development requires me to sit for long periods. Jogging helps me remain physically active and gives my body the exercise it needs.
Running also helps clear my mind. While jogging, I can temporarily forget about bugs, deadlines, and unfinished tasks. I can focus on my surroundings, my breathing, and the movement of my body.
Although I sometimes feel tired after work, I usually feel better after exercising. Jogging reduces my stress and helps me transition from my professional responsibilities to my personal time.
Cooking Dinner at Home
After jogging, I return home and prepare dinner. I usually cook my own food instead of depending on takeout or ready-made meals.

Cooking is another activity that helps me relax. It allows me to focus on something practical and creative that is unrelated to software development. The meal I prepare depends on the available ingredients and what I feel like eating that evening.
After exercising, having a freshly cooked meal feels especially rewarding. It gives me the energy I need and provides another moment to slow down after a long day.
Relaxing Before Bedtime

Once I have finished eating and cleaning up, I usually spend the rest of the evening relaxing. Depending on my mood, I may watch a movie. Sometimes, I choose an action or comedy movie, while on other nights, I may prefer something more relaxing.
Watching movies helps me rest and enjoy the final hours of the day. However, I do not always stay awake until late at night. My bedtime depends on how tired I feel.

On some days, I sleep at around 11:00 PM. If the day has been particularly tiring, I may go to bed as early as 9:00 PM. Getting enough rest is important, especially because I wake up very early the following morning.
Maintaining a Balanced Lifestyle
My daily routine may appear simple, but it provides a balance between my career, hobbies, physical health, and personal responsibilities.

Playing online games gives me time to have fun. Working at the office allows me to improve my skills and solve meaningful problems. Jogging keeps me active, while cooking and watching movies help me relax at home.

Being a software developer can be challenging, especially when I encounter complicated errors or urgent tasks. However, it can also be rewarding. Every problem I solve teaches me something new and helps me become a better developer.

At the end of the day, my life is not entirely about programming. It is also about enjoying my hobbies, taking care of my health, preparing my own meals, and giving myself enough time to rest. This routine helps me prepare for another day of challenges and opportunities.

profile.name: Pyanz Jheo C. Quiros
